metadata.summary: Go concurrency patterns: worker pool, rate limiter, semaphore, RWMutex cache. Channel ownership rule and explicit goroutine exit conditions. Race-detector required.

content.title: Go Concurrency Patterns
content.summary: Canonical Go goroutine and concurrency patterns: worker pool, rate limiter, semaphore, RWMutex cache, and sync.Once. The invariant: every goroutine must have an explicit exit condition (context.Done() or channel close), and the goroutine that writes to a channel is the one that closes it. Tests must pass go test -race.

why.p: Agents default to unbounded for { go work() } patterns, producing goroutine leaks invisible until production load. Missing context propagation and WaitGroup.Add-before-go misplacement are the most common silent correctness bugs. The race detector is mandatory but frequently skipped. These patterns encode the channel-ownership rule and leak-prevention idioms explicitly.

list.item: Generating a Go service that processes jobs in parallel (HTTP fan-out, batch ETL, queue consumer).
Refactoring sequential Go code into a worker pool with bounded concurrency.
Writing pipelines where stage N feeds stage N+1 via channels.
Adding context-based cancellation to existing goroutines.
Building rate limiters or semaphores around external APIs.
Single-threaded scripts or one-shot CLIs where startup cost dominates.
Code that needs to share mutable state without a clear ownership boundary — refactor the data model first.
Pure CPU-bound work with GOMAXPROCS=1 — goroutines add scheduling overhead with no parallelism.
When "concurrency" is really async I/O against one source — a single goroutine with select suffices.

rule.statement: The goroutine that writes to a channel is the one that closes it. No exceptions.
rule.rationale: Closing a channel from a reader or a different goroutine causes panic. Ownership rule eliminates the entire class of send-on-closed-channel bugs.

rule.statement: Every goroutine must have an explicit exit condition: ctx.Done(), channel close, or WaitGroup.Done path. Never write bare go func() without a shutdown mechanism.
rule.rationale: Goroutines without exit conditions leak indefinitely. Under production load this exhausts goroutine stack memory.

rule.statement: Use errgroup.WithContext (golang.org/x/sync) over hand-rolled WaitGroup + errCh for fan-out — it cancels siblings on first error automatically.
Bound concurrency with a buffered semaphore sized to downstream capacity (DB pool size, API rate limit), not to runtime.NumCPU().

section.name: rwmutex-cache

rule.statement: Use sync.RWMutex for read-heavy caches. Use sync.Map only when keys are write-once and reads dominate overwhelmingly; otherwise RWMutex + map is more predictable.

section.reference: TestMain with goleak for leak detection

antipattern.description: Generator goroutine without a ctx.Done() exit: go func() { for i := 0; ; i++ { ch <- i } }()
antipattern.reason: If no consumer reads from ch, the goroutine blocks forever. Memory and goroutine count grow unboundedly.

antipattern.description: Concurrent write to a plain map without a mutex: go func() { cache[key]++ }()
antipattern.reason: Concurrent map access is undefined behaviour in Go. The race detector catches it, but it may silently corrupt data or panic in production.

antipattern.description: Calling wg.Add(1) inside the goroutine body: go func() { wg.Add(1); defer wg.Done(); ... }()
antipattern.reason: The goroutine may not execute before wg.Wait() is called if the scheduler yields, causing premature completion.

rule.statement: Always call wg.Add(1) before launching the goroutine, never inside it.

section.name: race-detector-requirement

rule.statement: All tests for concurrency-heavy packages must pass go test -race -count=10 ./... before merge. Never skip the race detector.
rule.rationale: Concurrency bugs look correct under low load. The race detector and repeated runs surface them reliably.

section.reference: CI script enforcing race detector and staticcheck

template.purpose: TestMain with goleak.VerifyTestMain for leak detection in every concurrency-heavy package.
CI script: go vet, go test -race -count=3, staticcheck.
